reasoning: Heads-Up Poker Championship usually features 64 players. In a standard 64-player single elimination bracket, an individual player's baseline chance of winning is roughly 1/64 (1.56%). While skill variations impact this, poker tournaments have extremely high variance, making any one player an underdog to win the entire event. As an invited player, his inherent odds of running through 6 heads-up matches undefeated is low, typically around 1% to 2%. Without specific betting market odds suggesting he is a heavy favorite, the base rate for any single competitor winning this structured event rests around 1.5%.
